libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: No special environment variables or system properties are indicated. The observed failure is API drift in WebAppClassLoader.Context for Jetty 9.4.0.RC1: tests implementing that interface must use the current Class<?> and resource-aware methods, including isSystemClass(Class<?>), isServerClass(Class<?>), isServerResource(String, URL), and isSystemResource(String, URL).

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8856 [Automation] javac compile fails for org.eclipse.jetty:jetty-webapp:9.4.0.RC1; label=fails-javac-compile; body_chars=8015

risks: Tests that choose to exercise optional Jetty features such as JMX or JSP may require additional optional modules, but those are not necessary for meaningful jetty-webapp coverage.
Local Gradle compile/native-image verification remains authoritative because this is an RC-era Jetty API transition.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: No extra dependency, Docker image, or environment/system property is required. org.eclipse.jetty:jetty-webapp:9.4.0.RC1 brings the embedded Jetty webapp stack transitively, including jetty-servlet, jetty-security, jetty-server, jetty-http, jetty-io, jetty-util, jetty-xml, and javax.servlet-api. Meaningful coverage is reachable with embedded Server/WebAppContext tests using local files and loopback networking.
